sections.heading: 2024 Releases
sections.body:
- Supports the ability to read [Parquet](adf-parquet-data-format) files with
  [zstd](manual/reference/glossary) column compression.

* Adds support for programmatic workloads via OIDC (OpenID Connect) for Atlas Data Federation.
  To learn more, see [Workload Identity Federation (Applications)](oidc-authentication-workload).

* If you're an Admin user or a user with the [killop](manual/reference/privilege-actions) privilege,
  you can use the [killOp](manual/reference/command/killOp) command
  to [killOp](terminate-running-query) on a federated
  database. The user who issued the query can also terminate the query.

- Changes which regions process data for unsupported regions:

  - `ca-central-1 (Montreal)` will process data from `ca-west-1 (Alberta)`
    instead of `eu-west-1 (Ireland)`.

  - `ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo)` will process data:

    - from `ap-northeast-2 (Seoul)` instead of `eu-west-1 (Ireland)`.

    - from `ap-northeast-3 (Osaka)` instead of `eu-west-1 (Ireland)`.

    - from `ap-east-1 (Hong Kong)` instead of `ap-southeast-2 (Sydney)`.

- Fixes an issue where the [killOp](manual/reference/command/killOp)
  command was prevented from terminating a query.

* Adds support for the `$sql` stage on Atlas Data Federation views.

* Fixes an issue where, when creating a view, Atlas Data Federation was not
  properly checking permissions.

- Resolves an issue where generated dates in the ISO 8602 format caused incorrect query
  results.

* Fixes an issue that prevented the Python driver from failing to
  connect when using AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM authentication.

* Fixes an issue that caused [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) queries to fail with an
  `InternalError`.

* Improves template partition filtering in the query planner, which
  improves query performance in certain cases against multiple blob
  storage sources.

- Makes [Azure data stores](adf-configuration-file-azure)
  generally available for both your development and production
  deployments.

* Supports separate connection strings for private endpoint connections
  for [Atlas Data Federation](config-private-endpoint) and
  [Set Up a Private Endpoint for Online Archives](oa-config-private-endpoint).

- Supports AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM if you [AWS IAM Authentication](set-up-pwdless-auth) for authentication. This is currently unsupported
  in the Atlas UI.

sections.heading: 2023 Releases
sections.body:
- Changes the error `CommandNotSupported` to `CommandNotFound`.

* Supports OIDC (OpenID Connect) for Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, and Ping
  Identity as IdPs (Identity Providers). To learn more, see
  [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization).

- Improves performance when reading from
  [Parquet](adf-parquet-data-format) files.

- Adds support for modifying views with `collMod`.

- Adds automatic recognition of `.jsonl` files as JSON Lines files.

- Fixes an issue with [sqlGenerateSchema](sql-interface/schema/create)
  where it wouldn't run on Online Archive source.

* Adds a `lastModified` timestamp field to S3 (Simple Storage Service) object provenance metadata. To learn more,
  see [databases.\[n\].collections.\[n\].dataSources.\[n\].provenanceFieldName](datalakeconf-aws.databases.\[n].collections.\[n].dataSources.\[n].provenanceFieldName).

* Supports provenance metadata for Online Archive datasets.

- Improves error message for exceeding the [maxTimeMS](manual/reference/method/cursor.maxTimeMS) limit.

- Improves [explain() results](manual/reference/explain-results) for
  queries that target Atlas Data Lake datasets and Online Archives.

* Improves performance for queries that utilize the [$ne](manual/reference/operator/query/ne) operator.

- Allows you to set [read concern](manual/reference/read-concern)
  for [Atlas cluster](adf-configuration-file-atlas) data store.

* Supports the `$$SEARCH_META` aggregation variable when you run
  [$search](pipe.$search) queries on an Atlas cluster through
  Atlas Data Federation.

- Introduces new [onboarding experience](adf-getting-started) with
  templates for the most common Atlas Data Federation use cases.

- Improves error message for [$out](adf-out-stage) queries to provide more detail.

* Optimizes partition attributes for selecting files on S3 (Simple Storage Service) when
  using the [$in](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/in) operator
  in aggregation pipelines.

- Modifies the behavior of `$queryHistory` to indicate a query is
  complete when all batches have been uploaded as cursor files, all
  batches have been returned to the user, or there is an error.

- Adds the ability to use BSON data for the comment field in commands.

* Improves error messages when querying a document over 16MB.

* Fixes a correctness issue for [$getFields](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/getField) where Atlas Data Federation differed
  from MongoDB when querying an empty sub-document.

* Improves stability and performance for [$out](adf-out-stage) to
  S3 (Simple Storage Service) when writing to Parquet.

- Fixes an issue with `$match` queries that resulted in documents not being returned
  when querying on nested documents within an array where any nested document was missing
  the target field.

- Improves performance and stability when writing to Parquet using [$out](adf-out-stage) to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

- Adds the ability to use any BSON type with the `$comment` operator and query in
  `$queryHistory`. (Starting in MongoDB 5.1, the `$comment` operator
  was removed).

- Atlas Data Federation now returns MongoDB 6.2.0 in the [collStats](mql-support-diagnostic-cmd).

* Adds the ability to [limit the amount of data](adf-manage-query-limits) that Atlas Data Federation processes for your federated database instances to control costs.

- Improves error messages when a client attempts to insert,
  update, or delete a document in a federated database instance.

* Adds the application name to connections that Atlas Data Federation creates to your
  Atlas clusters.

* Adds the ability to set and update the storage configuration using the
  Atlas [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-data-federation).

- Fixes an issue that caused `maxTimeMS` with a `batchSize` of `0`
  to fail.

sections.heading: 2022 Releases
sections.body:
- Adds new capabilities to the storage configuration to support data provenance and improved flexibility for [Storage Configuration](config-adf).

- Adds [region](atlas-data-federation-regions) `ap-southeast-1` (Singapore).

* Updates Atlas Data Federation to MongoDB 6.0.2.

- Supports [killOp](terminate-running-query) for
  [collStats](manual/reference/command/collStats).

* Improves performance and stability.

* Improves query performance on Atlas Data Lake Datasets using sort metadata
  to optimize queries.

* Fixes an issue that caused Atlas Data Federation to fail to read a Parquet file
  when the top-level or root schema was marked as `REPEATED` or
  `OPTIONAL`.

* Improves stability when writing to Parquet using [$out](adf-out-stage)
  to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

- Fixes `$not` and `$in` pipeline issue that caused unsupported
  expression panic.

- Improves performance for `$out` to S3 (Simple Storage Service) queries that write to
  Parquet file format.

- Updates the default max row group size to 128MB for the parquet
  writer.

- Improves [$group](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/group) stages on Data Lake Dataset partition
  fields.

- Fixes aggregation pipelines with multiple `$lookup` stages where
  one stage defines a field and another removes the same field.

- Fixes how Atlas Data Federation handles files in S3 (Simple Storage Service) that end with the delimiter
  character (e.g. '/').

* Improves performance and stability.

* Adds support for optionally specifying an ISODate format to optimize
  performance for date-type partitions.

- Improves performance and stability.

- Performs [$merge](adf-merge-stage) in chunks.

* Improves performance and stability.

* Atlas now charges for the total number of bytes that Atlas Data Federation
  processes from HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) sources.

* Adds support for the background option on the [$merge](adf-merge-stage) aggregation stage.

- Improves performance and stability.

- Adds support for Atlas Data Lake as a "Store Type" to the `createStore` command.

- Improves error messaging for Federated `$search` queries.

* Renames and relaunches Atlas Data Lake as Atlas Data Federation.

  > **Important:** The federated query engine service previously called Atlas Data Lake is
  > now called Atlas Data Federation. To learn more about Atlas Data Federation, see
  > [federated database instances](atlas-data-federation).

- Improves performance and stability.

- Disables support for the MySQL dialect.

* Improves performance and stability.

- Improves performance and stability.

- Supports the following new MongoDB 5.2 aggregation operators:

  - `$sortArray`

  - `$topN`

  - `$bottomN`

  - `$maxN`

  - `$firstN`

  - `$lastN`

- Fixes a bug to allow you to use read preference for sharded clusters.

* Improves performance and stability.

- Improves performance and stability.

- Imposes an upper limit on
  `maxRowGroupSize`.

* Improves performance and stability.

* Renames the `matchComments` field to `queryFilterComments` . To
  learn more, see [Retrieve Data Lake Query History](adf-query-history-stage).

- Improves performance and stability.

- Adds `matchComments` field to query history. To learn more,
  see [Retrieve Data Lake Query History](adf-query-history-stage).

sections.heading: 2021 Releases
sections.body:
- Improves performance and stability.

- Supports queries on collections prefixed with `system`, but doesn't
  support queries on collections prefixed with `system.`.

* Improves performance and stability.

* Adds support for the
  [$maxTimeMS](manual/reference/operator/meta/maxTimeMS)
  option.

- Improves performance and stability.

- Allows connections to Data Lakes via
  [Private Endpoints](manage-private-endpoint).

- Adds support for X.509 authorization.

- Adds support for empty `field` parameters with the
  [$setField](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/setField)
  aggregation expression.

- Fixes an issue where commands returned zero exit codes on failure.

- Fixes an issue where documents with empty subdocuments
  written to Parquet contained empty parquet groups.

- Updates `EstimateRowGroupSize` to report `UncompressedSize` for
  documents stored in Parquet.

- Adjusts the minimum value for `maxRowGroupSize` when using
  [$out](adf-out-stage) to Parquet to 16MB.

- Removes support for using [$out](adf-out-stage) to write documents
  that contain duplicate fields to Parquet.

- Improves error messages for [$out](adf-out-stage).

* Improves performance and stability.

* Includes X.509 users in the [usersInfo](manual/reference/command/usersInfo) command output.

* Improves SCRAM authentication performance.

- Improves performance and stability.

- Adds support for the `authenticate` command.

- Preserves binary subtypes in the parquet reader/writer.

* Provides various stability and performance improvements.

* Adds support for `ap-south-1` [region](atlas-data-federation-regions).

* Outputs customer query logs into multiple lines.

* Includes `background` field in [$queryHistory output](adf-query-history-stage-output).

* Supports [wildcard databases and collections](generate-wildcard-collections) for Atlas data store.

- Provides various stability improvements.

- Improves `collStats` and `dbStats` [collStats](mql-support-diagnostic-cmd) performance and stability.

- Adds support for the [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) aggregation pipeline stage.

- Allows `localField` and `foreignField` with a more expressive
  $lookup aggregation
  pipeline stage syntax.

- Implements the `$count` accumulator.

* Improves performance.

* Improves error messaging.

* Adds `computeTime` and `automaticRefreshInProgress` fields to the
  `collStats` and `dbStats` [collStats](mql-support-diagnostic-cmd) outputs.

- Supports dropping non-existent [stores](adf-drop-store-cmd) and
  [databases](adf-drop-database-cmd) from the storage
  configuration.

- Includes `partitions.count` in [collStats](mql-support-diagnostic-cmd) command output.

* Allows downloading Data Federation query logs from the [UI](query-fdi) and [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-downloadgroupclusteronlinearchivequerylogs).

* Removes restriction on large collection namespaces.

* Adds option to bypass cache for [collStats](manual/reference/command/collStats) and [dbStats](manual/reference/command/dbStats) to fetch the most recent statistics.

* Supports [serverStatus](manual/reference/command/serverStatus)
  command.

- Improves stability and performance.

- Supports public S3 data stores with the `public` configuration flag.

- Supports Zstandard compression when federating queries to Atlas clusters.

- Adds `db` field to `dbStats` result.

* Supports selecting read preference, read tags, and max staleness
  through the [Storage Configuration](config-adf) for Atlas Cluster stores.

* Rejects commands sent with a Versioned API set.

* Enables the `count` parameter in the Data lake
  [$collStats Stage (Atlas Data Federation)](adf-collstats-stage) aggregation stage.

* No longer permits [$collStats Stage (Atlas Data Federation)](adf-collstats-stage) in `$facet`
  sub-pipelines.

* Enforces maximum document size for `$facet` after processing each
  item.

* Improves performance for `$match` stages.

* Improves error messaging.

- Improves stability and performance.

- Includes improved support for Parquet.

- Supports `M0`, `M2`, & `M5` Atlas clusters as data sources.

- Adds regex pattern matching option for wildcard collections from
  Atlas Clusters.

- Includes updated error messages for query execution limit.

* Generates storage configuration automatically for the first time
  after user authentication.

* Returns connection ID through the `hello` command.

* Supports `$geoNear` on Atlas Data Lake collections that span multiple
  Atlas clusters.

* Includes various performance improvements.

* Includes improved error messages for terminated queries.

- Includes new onboarding and storage configuration interface.

- Improved SQL schema error message.

- Support query pushdown to collections comprised of multiple Atlas
  collections.

- Improves stability and performance.

* Adds [SQL schema generation](sql-interface/query-with-sql) for wildcard
  collections.

* Fixes stability and performance issues.

- Adds a new [$sql](adf-sql-stage) `formatVersion` to reduce the data
  size of the result set.

- Improves performance of [$lookup Stage (Atlas Data Federation)](adf-lookup-stage).

- Adds `"verbosity": "queryPlannerExtended"` support to the
  [explain](manual/reference/command/explain) command to filter out
  non-matching partitions.

- Adds support for
  [$$NOW](manual/reference/aggregation-variables).

- Reports Atlas Data Lake as MongoDB version 4.4 to tools.

* Adds support for the background option on the
  [$out](adf-out-stage) aggregation stage.

* Includes stability and performance improvements.

sections.heading: 2020 Releases
sections.body:
- Adds `{background: true}` option, which allows queries to
  run in the background for [$out](adf-out-stage) to S3 (Simple Storage Service) stage.

- Introduces `$queryHistory` aggregation stage to view past
  queries.

- Includes various performance and stability improvements.

* Supports Parquet, CSV, and TSV formats for [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

* Adds a rolling limit for cursors.

* Improves error messages for commands that cannot be parsed.

- Supports the [$geoNear](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/geoNear) and [$graphLookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/graphLookup)
  [aggregation pipeline stages](manual/reference/operator/aggregation-pipeline) in queries on federated database instance
  collections that reference a single Atlas collection.

- Updates summary information in [explain](manual/reference/command/explain) output.

* Supports `defaultFormat` for files in publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s in
  HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) stores.

* Limits the number of simultaneous queries to 30 per federated database instance.

* Supports `bzip2` compression format.

* Supports `comment` option for the [aggregate](manual/reference/command/aggregate) command.

* Includes various performance and stability improvements.

- Supports [killOp](terminate-running-query) command for terminating
  a long-running query.

- Adds [configuration](datalakeconf-aws.databases.\[n].maxWildcardCollections)
  for maximum number of wildcard collections for S3 (Simple Storage Service) federated database instance stores.

* Supports [Storage Configuration](config-adf) as a
  data source.

* Supports AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) [Intelligent Tiering](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/storage-classes/#Unknown_or_changing_access)
  and [Standard-Infrequent Access](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/storage-classes/#Infrequent_access) storage
  classes.

* Supports [$unionWith](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/unionWith) aggregation stage.

* Restricts federated database instance [connection string](fdi-connect)
  authentication
  to one user at a time.

* Includes general performance and stability improvements.

- Improves [$out](adf-out-stage) to S3 (Simple Storage Service) write performance.

- Includes general performance and stability improvements.

* Adds `correlationID` to the [$currentOp](determine-query-status)
  output.

* Includes general performance and stability improvements.

- Relaxes [$out](adf-out-stage) S3 (Simple Storage Service) region requirement.

- Includes improved [Storage Configuration](config-adf)
  error messages.

- Includes general performance and stability improvements.

* Supports [$collStats](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/collStats) aggregation pipeline stage.

* Includes performance optimizations for [ORC](https://orc.apache.org/docs/)
  files.

* Includes general performance and stability improvements.

- Adds support for the `skip` and `limit` fields to the `count()`
  command.

* Adds `storageValidateConfig` command to validate your
  federated database instance [Storage Configuration](config-adf).

* Includes bug fixes and performance improvements.

- Includes general performance and stability improvements.

* Adds support for [Atlas Clusters](atlas/scale-cluster) as a
  data source.

* Improves performance for the [$lookup Stage (Atlas Data Federation)](adf-lookup-stage) aggregation pipeline stage.

* Adds support for evaluating string [$convert](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/convert) expressions in the `filename`
  for [$out](adf-out-stage) to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

* Updates [Parquet](https://parquet.apache.org/docs/)
  support for [MAP types](https://github.com/apache/parquet-format/blob/master/LogicalTypes.md#nested-types).

* Improves error messaging for [$out](adf-out-stage) to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

* Adds a command to generate a storage configuration.

- Automates storage configuration generation for newly created
  federated database instances.

- Allows write partitioning-aware data to S3 using the [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out)
  in Data Federation.

* Generates Storage Configs when Atlas creates a federated database instance.

* Adds support for [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

* Updates support for [Apache Parquet](https://parquet.apache.org/)
  [LIST](https://github.com/apache/parquet-format/blob/master/LogicalTypes.md#lists)
  element.

* Upgrades [wire protocol](manual/reference/mongodb-wire-protocol)
  support to 4.2 from 3.6.

* Adds support for verbosity in the explain plan.

- Fixes stability issues.

* Improves performance.

* Supports the [$currentOp](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/currentOp)
  stage so that you can monitor query progress on long-running queries.

* Updates the [isodate](adf-path-attribute-types) attribute
  to accept additional formats.

* Refreshes the metadata catalog when you use
  [Storage Configuration](config-adf) commands.

- Includes various performance and stability improvements.

- Supports filename field references for [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out).

- Supports [$toString](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/toString)
  in [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) to S3 (Simple Storage Service).

* Supports optionally granting federated database instance write access to S3
  buckets, enabling use of [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) semantics to write directly
  to those buckets.

* Adds incremental [view](adf-cli), [view](adf-cli),
  [view](adf-cli), and [view](adf-cli)
  commands for storage configuration management.

* Limits collections returned for wildcard collections to 1,000.

* Updates the storage configuration format.

- Supports cross-database [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) queries.

- Supports lowercase and uppercase file extensions.

- Template segments now support dot-separated attribute names that
  correspond to nested fields.

* Allows the [defaultFormat](adf-aws-databases-reference)
  to be specified without a leading dot.

* Supports filtering based on stripes for files in ORC format.

* Allows query attributes to be extracted after the first stage.

sections.heading: 2019 Releases
sections.body:
- Includes several performance and stability improvements.

- Supports partition definition for the following:

  - `epoch_secs`, which is seconds since the Unix Epoch

  - `epoch_millis`, which is milliseconds since the Unix Epoch

  - `UUID`, which is binary subtype 4

* Includes several performance and stability improvements.

* Adds support for reading Apache ORC files.

- Supports filtering partitions by [Parquet file row group](https://parquet.apache.org/docs/) statistics.

- Supports [ObjectIds](manual/reference/method/ObjectId) in the
  path when specifying partition
  `databases.<database>.<collection>.[n].definition`.

* Returns an error if a query produces a document larger than 16 MiB.

* The [$indexStats](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/indexStats) stage now produces an empty list of indexes instead
  of an error.

* Supports [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) to S3 storage format in JSON.

* [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match) now implicitly treats all terms as conjunctions.

* No longer parses empty files.

* Fixes an issue that caused the `{$match: {$expr: {$and: []}}}` expression
  to terminate the connection.

- Allows nested fields in partition [definitions](adf-path-syntax).

- No longer enumerates directories on S3 when a single subdirectory containing
  all the partitions matching the query is identified.

- Fixes an issue where the new storage configuration did not appear
  on the issuing connection after running [setStorageConfig](adf-setstorageconfig).

* Adds support for the [getLastError](manual/reference/command/getLastError) database command.

* Fixes a bug with how union types are handled in Avro.

* Supports [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) aggregation pipeline stage to S3.

* [listIndexes](manual/reference/command/listIndexes) now always returns an empty list.

* Translates dot-delimited CSV and TSV keys into subdocuments.

* Storage configuration error message now includes a link to the
  documentation.

* Supports the XLSX file format.

* Includes the correlation ID in query execution error messages.

* Returns an error to the client when the cursor storage limit is reached.

* Returns an error to the client on the last [getMore](manual/reference/command/getMore) if the cursor
  storage limit is exceeded.

- Supports [listCommands](manual/reference/command/listCommands). For example: `db.runCommand({"listCommands": 1})`

- Includes partition size information in the output of [explain()](manual/reference/method/db.collection.explain).

* Returns the first batch of cursor results more quickly.

* Improves performance of [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) when combined with [$unwind](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/unwind).

* Automatically supports `SCRAM-SHA-1` credentials without requiring drivers
  to specify this authentication mechanism.

* Provides a descriptive error message when the file format is unknown.

* Provides additional validation on [setStorageConfig](adf-setstorageconfig).

Initial public preview release of [federated database instances](atlas-data-federation).
